sun.hasta_rekha: Ravi Rekha (Sun line), Ravi-sthana (Mount of Sun)
sun.associated_deity: Śiva, Agni (Śiva = the destroyer, Agni = fire god)
sun.mantra: AUM SŪRYAYA NAMAHA or AUM GHRINI SŪRYAYA NAMAHA – chanted 108 times at sunrise on Sundays

moon.hasta_rekha: Chandra-sthana (Mount of Moon), Chandra Rekha (Moon line)
moon.mantra: AUM SOM SOMAYA NAMAHA – chanted 108 times at sunrise on Monday

mercury.hasta_rekha: Budha-sthana (Mount of Mercury), Budha Rekha (Mercury line)

health_vitality: majjā (bone marrow; see 'Seven Tissues (Sapta Dhātu)' in Chapter 13)
mars.hasta_rekha: Mangala-sthana (Mount of Mars), Mangala Rekha (Mars line)
mars.gemstone: Red coral (primary), red spinel, red carnelian or red agate (substitute)
mars.mantra: AUM KUJAYA NAMAHA or AUM KRAN KRIN KRON SAH BHAUMAYA NAMAH – chanted 108 times at sunrise on Tuesday

jupiter.hasta_rekha: Favourable Jupitarian signs: well-developed Tarjani (forefinger), Guru Chandrika, Diksha Rekha and Jupiter-sthana
jupiter.associated_deity: Indrā (king of the gods, wielder of lightning)
jupiter.mantra: AUM BRIM BRAHASPATYA NAMAHA - chanted 108 times at sunrise on Thursday

venus.hasta_rekha: Shukra-sthana (Mount of Venus), Shukra Rekha (Venus line)
venus.associated_deity: Indrāni (consort to Indrā, queen of the gods)
venus.mantra: AUM SHUKRAN SHUKRAYA NAMAHA - chanted 108 times at sunrise on Friday

natural_world: any creature that has poison associated with its bite
environments: places with animals held in captivity
occupations: Farmers and those who tend the land
saturn.hasta_rekha: Shani-sthana (Mount of Saturn), Shani Rekha (Fate line)

rahu.mantra: AUM RAM RAHAVE NAMAHA – chanted 108 times at sunrise on Saturday

ketu.mantra: AUM KE KETAVE NAMAHA - chanted 108 times at sunrise on Tuesday

ashwini.karma_karakas: Preparation of medicine, use of healing mantra, practice of astrology, construction of yantra, worship of guru/teacher. Travel, moving home, conveyances: including four-legged animals such as horses and elephants
ashwini.devata_deity: Ashwini Kumaras: horse-headed twins, acknowledged for their healing skills and rejuvenative arts
ashwini.postconventional_items: Gold, black cloth, black gram, black sesame seed paste, Garuda (eagle), Kuchala,¹³ lotus flower, white flowers, Sudha (calcium carbonate), sandalwood¹⁴ or guggulu¹⁵ incense, milk or milk-rice pudding.
ashwini.ahuti_fire_ritual: Milk-rice pudding, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
ashwini.description: Those offering horses, chariots/carriages under this star incur a future noble birth

bharani.karma_karakas: Purification of sacred space with fire, removal of negative forces, construction of puṭa (burning pits), calcination of metals and minerals, drying of herbs, purgation of toxins, surgery, imprisonment, accidents, war (killing), gambling, theft, winning women and adventures
bharani.devata_deity: Yamarāj: lord of death and the underworld, he and his sister (Yami) become the first humans to ascend to Devaloka (godly realm)
bharani.postconventional_items: Blue cloth, blue lotus, Amalaki,¹⁶ Kāka (crow), Guggulu, Aguru¹⁷ incense and jaggery rice
bharani.ahuti_fire_ritual: Honey and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
bharani.description: Those offering land or cattle to Brahmins under this star receive large numbers of cattle in a future birth
health_conditions: accidents or injury to the neck and face

krittika.chinha_symbol: Arrow in flight, sword, razor or edged weapon
krittika.karma_karakas: All fire rituals, worship of Lord Śiva, forging, sharpening metal, heating, burning, cutting, tearing, purifying, preparation of remedies that improve speech and intelligence, gaining siddhis through sādhanā¹⁸
krittika.devata_deity: Agni: fire god. A pivotal figure in Vedic literature, firstly co-creationist of the universe, later adopting the role of celestial messenger
krittika.postconventional_items: Fig tree, Myura (peacock), honey, ghee, all red flowers but especially Karaveera,¹⁹ ghee and guggulu incense
krittika.ahuti_fire_ritual: Yoghurt and cooked rice, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
krittika.description: Milk-rice prepared for the satisfaction of Brahmin under this star, attain merit in a future birth
health_conditions: Prone to irregular elimination (constipation or bouts of diarrhoea)

rohini.tara_stars: α, θ1, θ2, γ, δ and ε Tauri
rohini.karma_karakas: Planting herbs and spices, purveying of wholesome foods, manufacture of perfumes, extraction of essential oils, construction, laying foundation stones, Vāstu (arrangement of sacred space), buying new homes, marriage, vehicles and the promotion of leadership
rohini.devata_deity: Prajapati: an epithet of Brahmā, frequently described as lord of all living creatures. Brahmā is first of three supreme divinities in Hinduism (collectively known as Trimūrti).
rohini.postconventional_items: Sugarcane, Jambu,20 lunar symbol, Hamsa (swan/goose), Sarasvatī, black cloth and flowers, black sesame seed paste, Brahmā statue, Tamala leaf,21 Salai Guggulu,22 milk rice, Kasturi23 gland
rohini.ahuti_fire_ritual: Mustard seeds and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
rohini.description: Milk-rice prepared with ghee and served to Brahmins ends all indebtedness to ones ancestors

punarvasu.karma_karakas: Fasting, dietary changes, intake of foods rich in minerals, harvesting fruits, Āyurvedic study, taking medicines, Jyotish, Seemantham (ritual undertaken by pregnant women in odd-term months), educational rituals, new vehicles or travel
punarvasu.devata_deity: Āditi: mother to the twelve Ādityas, her name means ‘boundless’ or ‘infinity’
punarvasu.postconventional_items: Black cloth, bamboo, Hamsa (swan), mantra, boiled milk, saffron, white sandalwood paste, jasmine and jaggery
punarvasu.ahuti_fire_ritual: Sesame rice and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
punarvasu.description: Those offering bread on the day of this star are awarded birth into a wealthy family

magha.tara_stars: α, η, γ1, ζ, μ, ε and o Leonis
magha.karma_karakas: Consulting learned individuals, formulating strategies, gambling, taking risks, all decisive actions, Pitri Tarpana,30 Kanyadhanam,31 recitation of snake bite mantra, digging wells
magha.devata_deity: Pitris/Manes: progenitors/forefathers of mankind, warding over the fates of mankind
magha.pooja_ceremonial_items: Vata (Ficus bengalensis), Champak (Magnolia champaca), black cloth, Kāka (crow), golden Pitru statue, Aguru, ghee and Guggulu incense
magha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Ghee, wheat flour and jaggery cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
magha.nakshatrayoga: Alms given on the day of this star are rewarded with cattle

purva_phalguni.karma_karakas: Acts of self-promotion (fame, mass media, etc.), artistic performances, visual presentations, protection of wealth, performance of black magic, climatic disturbances, threats to colonies (epidemics/pandemics/contamination)
purva_phalguni.devata_deity: Bhaga: one of twelve Ādityas, brother to Uṣas (first light)
purva_phalguni.postconventional_items: White and red flowers, Palasha (Butea monosperma) and Karaveera (Nerium oleander), Garuda (eagle), red sandalwood, Bilwa wood (Aegle marmelos) incense
purva_phalguni.ahuti_fire_ritual: Cooked chickpeas, mustard seeds and Priyangu (Callicarpa microphylla) cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
purva_phalguni.description: Those offering rice and ghee on the day of this star attain happiness and prosperity

uttara_phalguni.karma_karakas: Throne rooms, temples, boundaries of colonies, marriages, education, economics (money-making), leadership, agriculture, entering a new home, planting herbs, taking sustenance (nurturing), treatment of diseases, auspicious Nakshatra for performing mantra and other religious rituals
uttara_phalguni.devata_deity: Aryaman: one of twelve Ādityas and devotee of Sûrya (Sun)
uttara_phalguni.postconventional_items: Plaksha (Ficus uliginosa), blue lotus (Nymphaea caerulea), red cloth, sindoora (red lead/cinnabar), Zatacchada (Indian woodpecker32) red rice, Guggulu or mustard seeds
uttara_phalguni.ahuti_fire_ritual: Badara (Ziziphus mauritiana) or Priyangu (Callicarpa microphylla) cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
uttara_phalguni.description: Offerings of navara33 rice and ghee on the day of this star make one honoured in heaven

chitra.karma_karakas: Planting herbs, landscaping (ornamental gardens), architecture, engineering, construction of yantra, wearing of astrological pendants, acquisition of jewels, mining gemstones, Vedic studies, learning, Hastā-mudrā (ritual hand gestures), painting, graphic design, marriage, purchasing or tending elephants and horses
chitra.devata_deity: Twastar: artificer to the gods, given the title Viśvarūpa (omniform) for his ability to manifest all manner of beings by will alone
chitra.postconventional_items: Multicoloured cloth, ornaments, jewels, white objects, Bilwa (Aegle marmelos), Baka (crane), golden statue of Twastar, saffron, sandalwood, multicoloured flowers, wheat flower and jaggery cooked as a pudding, Shala niryasa (Shorea robusta) aromatic resin incense
chitra.ahuti_fire_ritual: Chitrānna35 (flavoured rice), cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
chitra.description: Perfumery offered on the day of this star grant access to the celestial gardens in which maidens dance and play

shravana.karma_karakas: Spiritual devotion, Yagya, pooja, Vrata (devotion), Nirāhāra (fasting), mantra, renunciation, initiation, making music, preparation of medicinal remedies (especially Rasāyana formulae), education, Gārbha-ādana,45 ear piercing and Shanti-homam ritual
shravana.devata_deity: Vishnu: Vishnu means ‘highest step’. His rulership over this star places considerable emphasis over its role within the asterisms. Vishnu is the second of three supreme divinities in Hinduism (collectively known as Trimūrti)
shravana.pooja_ceremonial_items: Black cloth, Aswatha (Ficus religiosa), Jala Kukkuti (black-headed gull), black statue of Vishnu, saffron, sandalwood, mustard seed, Tulsi (Ocimum tenuiflorum), red flowers, Dhashaga Guggulu (ten aromatic herbs), cooked shali rice cooked and offered with mantra to Vishnu, milk rice pudding made with red rice
shravana.ahuti_fire_ritual: Aswatha wood and Lāja, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
shravana.nakshatrayoga: Those offering rugs or cloth on the day of this star are carried by white conveyances freely entering heaven

shatabhishak.tara_stars: λ, τ, δ, η, ζ, γ and α Aquarii
shatabhishak.karma_karakas: Āyurveda/rasāyana therapies, visha shodhana (purification of poison), such as narcotic/hallucinogenic herbs, alchemy/Rasa Shāstra, digging of wells, walled gardens, agriculture, travel, gemstones and coral, collections, marriages, charity and the training of horses
shatabhishak.devata_deity: Varuna: a powerful oceanic deity featuring prominently in early Vedic poems
shatabhishak.postconventional_items: Red cloth, khadira (Acacia catechu), Kokila (Jacobin cuckoo), golden statue of Varuna, malayaja sandalwood,⁴⁸ blue lotus, ghee and Guggulu incense, Māmsa (meat of deer or goat) offered to fire,⁴⁹ puja with offerings of honey and ghee
shatabhishak.ahuti_fire_ritual: Rice milk pudding and white flowers, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
shatabhishak.description: Offerings of aloe or sandalwood made on the day of this star aid one to abide in the Deva realm

revati.karma_karakas: Jyotish, deity worship, consecration of yantra, preparation of healing remedies, wearing gemstones, planting herbs, wearing of sacred thread, marriages, construction of sacred spaces (temples), acquiring horses or elephants
revati.devata_deity: Pushan: one of twelve Ādityas, this deity was considered as overseer/ protector of travellers and sustainer/guardian of cattle
revati.postconventional_items: Red cloth, butter tree (Madhuca indica), Myura (peacock), golden statue of Pūṣan, red saffron, Arka (Calotropis gigantea), Guggulu incense, Navadhana (nine types of grain52), cooked rice, saffron and red flowers
revati.ahuti_fire_ritual: Sesame, rice and fruits, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
revati.description: Those offering cattle or pots of milk on the day of this star are granted safe passage to all lands

ardra.karma_karakas: Purification and detoxification (purgation of visha/poison), defeat of enemies, exorcism of spirits, witnessing terrible things (trauma, struggles, torture, violence, accidents involving fire, etc.), worship of Rudra, overcoming the fear of death, feeding white cows
ardra.devata_deity: Rudra: an early Vedic storm deity, presiding over rain, cattle and health. Rudra later becomes synonymous with a wrathful incarnation of Śiva. Śiva is third of three supreme divinities in Hinduism (collectively known as Trimūrti)
ardra.postconventional_items: White flowers, white sandalwood paste, Dattura,25 white sesame seed paste, Khadira, Krouncha (heron), sesame paste, shali (red rice) Aguru
ardra.ahuti_fire_ritual: Honey and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
ardra.description: Offerings of sesame seed oil, ghee or abstinence from food on the day of this star ensure safe passage on perilous journeys

anuradha.karma_karakas: Yogic practice, mantra, developing Siddhi (supernatural powers) diagnosis of disease (root causes), manufacture of medicine, testing gold and gemstones, purchasing vehicles, collecting animal horns, acquisition of cows, bulls and elephants, safe journeys and long lasting marriages
anuradha.devata_deity: Mitra: known as a sustainer of gods, his role appears mostly supportive, stabilising the space between the Earth, Sun and heavens
anuradha.postconventional_items: Red cloth, Panasa/Jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus), Śuka Pakshi or Raktapada (parrot), golden statue of Mitra, lotus flowers, Sabji (red banana flower), Bilwa wood incense
anuradha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Red milk rice sweetened with jaggery, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
anuradha.description: Those giving clothes, fabric or rice on the day of this star are honoured in heaven for one hundred Yugas

ashlesha.tara_stars: ε, δ, σ, η, ρ and ζ Hydrae
ashlesha.karma_karakas: Practice or study of Jyotish, mantra against black magic, treatment of infection, removal of poisons, treatment of paralysis or viral illness, Ratnachikitsa (especially emerald), preparation of Maṇi bhasma, medicinal Visha (healing poisons), blaming, depression and suicide
ashlesha.devata_deity: Sarpa: one of eleven Rudras (Ekādaśarudras), Sarpa was the offspring of Tvashtar, artificer to the gods
ashlesha.pooja_ceremonial_items: Nagakeshara,28 Tittibha (red-wattled lapwing bird), honey, red cloth (red-brown like the fur of a monkey), Haridra29 root, brown/ red flowers, a serpent idol, sandalwood paste, rice pudding and sweetened milk
ashlesha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Ghee and Guggulu, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
ashlesha.nakshatrayoga: Silver attained on this day makes one fearless

dhanishtha.tara_stars: β, α, γ and δ Delphini
dhanishtha.karma_karakas: Wearing gemstones, Yajnopaveeta (sacred thread), medicinal treatment (including surgery), burning, cutting and forging of metal, buying ornaments, vehicles or cloth, nourishment, building temples, royal courts, Upanayana Samskara (acceptance ritual)
dhanishtha.devata_deity: Vasus: a collective of eight important deities: Soma (Moon), Dharā (Earth), Anila (wind), Uṣas (dawn), Anala (fire), Dhṛuva (pole star), Pratyūsa (pre-dawn) and Parjanya (god of thunderstorms)
dhanishtha.postconventional_items: White cloth, white flowers, Tummeda (bumble bee), golden statue of the Vasus, sandalwood, Jati/jasmine flowers (Jasminum grandiflorum), Guggulu, Sûrya maṇdala, wood of the fig tree, mung beans, red rice and aromatic flowers
dhanishtha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Milk and jaggery, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
dhanishtha.description: Those offering clothes, fabrics or cattle on the day of this star are eligible to freely enter heaven

hasta.tara_stars: δ, γ, ε, α, β and η Corvi
hasta.karma_karakas: Empowering gemstones, Hastā Rekha Shāstra, Jyotish, magic, massage therapy, preparation (grinding) of herbs, Hastā/Paṇi Grahanam³⁴ (taking of the hand), travel, leadership, new vehicles, new homes, jewels, establishing new kingdoms
hasta.devata_deity: Savitar: one of twelve Ādityas, charged with the conveyance of souls into the afterlife
hasta.postconventional_items: Red cloth, red flowers, red sandalwood, Plaksha (Ficus uliginosa), Amratakan (Spondias pinnata), Śuka Pakshi or Raktapada (parrot), golden statue of Sûrya, Dashanga Guggulu (a mixture of ten aromatic resins/gums) for incense purposes
hasta.ahuti_fire_ritual: Wheat flour and jaggery pudding, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
hasta.description: One obtains worldly bliss offering a horse or elephant on the day of this star

jyeshtha.karma_karakas: Personal challenges, acts of faith, courage, affording protection to the weak or helpless, surgery, war, weapons, winning over opponents, conquering new lands, political gains, surrender, wild horses and elephants (stampeded out of control)
jyeshtha.devata_deity: Indrā: primarily a thunder deity, Indra's secondary roles included dispelling darkness and the removal of drought through the liberation of rain
jyeshtha.postconventional_items: Betel nut tree (Piper betel), Kokila (Jacobin cuckoo), rice and sesame seeds (cooked), all yellow flowers, sandalwood, Pātala³⁷ (Steriospermum suvaveolens) or black eagle wood incense
jyeshtha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Chitrānna with ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
jyeshtha.description: Those offering yams or green vegetables to a Brahmin on the day of this star realise all their wishes

moola.tara_stars: λ, υ, ι, κ, θ and η Scorpionis
moola.chinha_symbol: Elephant goad, roots of a herb, lion’s tail
moola.karma_karakas: Planting herbs, wholesome food (especially root vegetables), Jyotish, mantra, surgery, marriages, new homes, building Aushadhi (pharmacies) and pilgrimages
moola.devata_deity: Niriti: guardian of the south west and daughter to Adharma (immorality) and Himsa (violence), better known as the goddess of dissolution and destruction
moola.pooja_ceremonial_items: Red cloth, black ornaments, climbing plants (creepers) or tubers, Chakra Vaka (brahmany duck), black statue of Niriti, black gram cooked with red saffron and ghee, black eagle wood, blue louts flower, Ajasrnga (goat horn) / Salai Guggulu (Boswellia serrata) or Shala (Shorea robusta) for burning/incense, sandalwood
moola.ahuti_fire_ritual: Black gram, fruits, sandalwood and flowers, cast into fire 108 times with Gayatri mantra
moola.nakshatrayoga: Offerings of roots or nuts to a Brahmin on the day of this star help pacify ones ancestors

mrigashira.karma_karakas: Manufacture of rejuvenating medicine, rasāyana therapy, making ghee, collecting milk, honey, dates and almonds, yogic practice, bathing under moonlight, sādhanā, charitable acts, promoting a business
mrigashira.devata_deity: Soma: presiding over amrita (nectar), this deity takes on a number of guises in the Vedas, named and honoured, yet less anthropomorphic than his contemporaries. Soma later becomes synonymous with the Moon god – Chandra
mrigashira.pooja_ceremonial_items: Gold, white cloth, white flowers, lotus, Khadira,24 Kukkuti (hen), Shadanga-dupa (six aromatic herbs, gums and resins), milk rice
mrigashira.ahuti_fire_ritual: Milk rice and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
mrigashira.nakshatrayoga: One attains heaven if dairy cattle are offered on this day of this star

pushyami.chinha_symbol: Arrow head, flower or cow’s udder
pushyami.karma_karakas: Study of Vedas (scriptures), teaching, mantra, Jyotish, Āyurveda, worshipping of astra (weapons), removal of curses, pooja and attainment of siddhis
pushyami.devata_deity: Brihaspati: instructor to the gods and Vedic warrior (armed with an iron axe). Later Brihaspati becomes identified with the planet god Jupiter
pushyami.postconventional_items: White cloth and flowers, Aswatha, Jala Murgābī (waterhen), yellow cloth, saffron and sandalwood, lotus flowers, burning Khadira wood (Acacia catechu) and rice yoghurt
pushyami.ahuti_fire_ritual: Milk and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
pushyami.description: Gold, given on the day of this star ensures the pleasure of all planets

purvabhadrapada.karma_karakas: Education, cleansing with salt water, Vamana (vomiting), gemstone purification, sacred mantra, use herbal medicines, digging of wells, construction, agriculture/irrigation, planting herbs, construction of yantra, tantric practices, removing enemies and gambling
purvabhadrapada.devata_deity: Aja-ekapād: meaning ‘single-footed goat’ – perhaps connected to a number of aerial/storm deities such as Rudra or Ahi-budhnya
purvabhadrapada.postconventional_items: Black cloth, Shamali (Salmalia malabarica⁵⁰), Kapota (pigeon), golden statue of Aja-ekapād, red saffron, Arka (Calotropis gigantea), Guggulu (incense), cooked rice, yoghurt and flowers
purvabhadrapada.ahuti_fire_ritual: Kushmanda (ash pumpkin) and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
purvabhadrapada.description: Those offering coins on the day of this star obtain much bliss

purvashadha.tara_stars: δ, ε, η and λ Sagittarii
purvashadha.karma_karakas: Hydrotherapy, agriculture, soil fertilisation, sowing seeds, deep wells, lakes, preparation of herbal remedies, Rasāyana therapies, use of protective mantra, Graha tantra⁴⁰ (demonology), weapons, training with weapons, Pitru Paksha
purvashadha.devata_deity: Apas: goddess of water and one of the eight Vasus in the service of Indrā
purvashadha.postconventional_items: White cloth, Baka (crane), white sandalwood (Chandana⁴¹), Kaluva lotus (Nelumbo nucifera), Tintrini/tamarind (Tamarindus indica), Manasheela (realgar), Kankusta (Garcinia cambogia), rice, milk and brown sugar pudding
purvashadha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Laaja and flowers, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
purvashadha.description: Pots of curd given to Brahmin (after fasting), help one attain birth into a family owning many cows, on the day of this star

swati.karma_karakas: Harvesting medicinal plants, tending botanical gardens and leisure parks, treatment of disease, Jyotish, Rasa Shāstra (alchemy), work that requires precision (making pillars for temples), swiftness, flexibility, riding in chariots, education, studying for exams, women in fifth month of pregnancy, Yajnopaveeta (sacred thread ritual)
swati.devata_deity: Vāyu: personification of the wind, as distinguished from Vāta (dosha). Vāyu was an important deity in Rig Veda, commonly evoked as the dual deity Indrā-Vāyu
swati.postconventional_items: Ornaments, red cloth (ruby red), Garuda (eagle) Arjuna (Terminalia arjuna), black eagle wood or sandalwood incense, golden statue of Vāyu
swati.ahuti_fire_ritual: Laaja (puffed) rice, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
swati.description: Alms of any sort given on the day of this star are renowned to grant benefits and reward

uttarabhadrapada.karma_karakas: Worship of deities, construction of sacred buildings, construction of yantra, planting herbs, potentising gems, sculptures, marriages, fire rituals, wearing new clothes
uttarabhadrapada.devata_deity: Ahi-budhnya: thought to be inspired by a particular form of atmospheric phenomena, detailed description of its form is lacking. Ahi-budhnya is sometimes referred to as a serpent in the depths
uttarabhadrapada.postconventional_items: White cloth, Pichumanda (neem wood51), Ulūka (owl), golden statue of Ahi-budhnya, white flowers, rose flower, Barbara (wild basil), Guggulu and ghee (incense)
uttarabhadrapada.ahuti_fire_ritual: Cooked rice, jaggery and milk, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
uttarabhadrapada.description: Those offering mutton on the day of this star bring pleasure to the Manes (progenitors)

uttarashadha.karma_karakas: Planting/growing herbs, treatment of diseases, defeat of ones enemy, pilgrimages, education, gardens, plants and forests, divine worship, kings, rulers and leaders, anna prashana, temples, auspicious undertakings
uttarashadha.devata_deity: Vishvadevas: a collective of minor gods and goddesses that included Vasus, Âdityas, Rudras, Maruts, Aṅgirasas and Ṛbhus
uttarashadha.postconventional_items: White colour, white sandalwood, Pañca Varna Pushpa, Kokila (Jacobin cuckoo), wood of Amla (Emblica officinalis), Pañca Bhakshya (sweet bread), golden idol of Vishvadevas
uttarashadha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Rice and ghee, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
uttarashadha.description: When milk and ghee (sweetened with honey) are given to a wise man on the day of this star, the individual attains honours in heaven

vishakha.tara_stars: ι, α, β and σ Librae
vishakha.karma_karakas: Spiritual practice, gemstone cutting and wearing, taking medicine, healing/therapies, overcoming diseases, seasonal agriculture, harvesting, digging wells and the constructing water tanks
vishakha.devata_deity: Indrā-Agnī: most venerated of all dualistic deities, many exhalant hymns are given in praise of Indrā-Agnī (Vedic warrior gods)
vishakha.postconventional_items: Kapitha36 (Limonia acidissima), Ulūka (owl), golden statue of Indrā-Agnī, oil of Devadaru (Cedrus deodara) for incense, Shatapatri (rose petals), red saffron, wheat flour and jaggery pudding
vishakha.ahuti_fire_ritual: Milk rice sweetened with jaggery, cast into fire 108 times with Gāyatrī mantra
vishakha.description: Those offering diamonds, cattle, milk or ghee on the day of this star attain heaven

shukra_5.description: Bones/joints, Brain tissue/marrow and nerve tissue, Semen/ovum (reproductive fluids)

astrological_data.respective_fingers: Middle Finger or Little Finger (left hand)
